text.title: AIOps Implementation and Audit Checklists
text.summary: Checklists for anomaly detection setup, RCA, incident management, auto-remediation policy, platform selection, and quarterly audit.

list.item: Metrics coverage: CPU, memory, disk, network, latency, error rates all instrumented
Log aggregation: centralized with structured (JSON) format
Distributed tracing enabled across all services
Establish 2+ weeks of baseline data before enabling ML alerts
Map seasonality: weekly, monthly, and release-day patterns
Select algorithm: Isolation Forest for general anomalies, Prophet for time-series, LSTM for complex patterns
Use dynamic thresholds, not static; connect to SLO error-budget burn rates
Target false positive rate below 20% before expanding alert coverage
Define escalation paths for unacknowledged alerts
Build feedback loop: process for teams to mark false positives for model retraining
Maintain up-to-date service dependency graph (topology)
Track change events: deployments, config changes, feature flag flips
Configure correlation window for event grouping (typically 5-30 minutes)
Link APM traces to RCA findings
Attach relevant logs and anomalous metrics to each incident
Output ranked probable causes with confidence percentages
Export RCA data for postmortem templates
Define scope: explicit list of what can and cannot be auto-remediated
Classify each action by risk level (low/medium/high)
Set approval matrix: which roles can approve medium and high risk actions
Define blast-radius limits: maximum impact of any single auto-action
Set rate limits: maximum actions per time window per service
Set cooldown periods: minimum time between same action on same target
Require health verification after each auto-action (confirm improvement)
Log all actions with context for audit trail
Provide manual override to disable auto-remediation per service
MTTR trend: improving quarter-over-quarter?
False positive rate: decreasing or stable?
Auto-remediation rate: coverage expanding as planned?
Concept drift: retrain models if prediction accuracy declining
Runbook accuracy: do runbooks match current system topology?
Feature relevance: are health-score inputs still predictive?
Tool adoption: are teams using AIOps capabilities in incident response?
